name: oss-best-practices-research
description: Research current community best practices for MCP/CLI-dispatcher projects, then synthesize concrete, high-leverage recommendations grounded in this repo.

phases.name: research
phases.prompt: You have access to real-time web search tools. Use them. Research current (2024-2025) community best practices for local-first CLI/agent dispatcher and MCP (Model Context Protocol) server projects similar to OpenCode, Aider, Continue, and Cline. Specifically investigate and CITE REAL SOURCES (with URLs) for: 1) Architecture patterns for multi-provider CLI routing/broker tools (adapter pattern, capability-based routing, plugin systems). 2) MCP server design conventions and emerging ecosystem standards (tool naming, schema validation, error envelopes, resource vs tool design). 3) Open-source contribution practices: CONTRIBUTING.md structure, PR/issue templates, semantic versioning & changesets, CI gating strategies. 4) Testing & validation methodologies for CLI-process-spawning tools (mocking subprocess calls, golden-file testing, live-validation/smoke-testing patterns, sandboxing). 5) Documentation & discoverability practices that help adoption (README structure, examples, capability matrices). Write a thorough markdown report to the file '{{inputs.researchFile}}' in the current working directory, creating parent directories if needed. Include concrete URLs/sources you found. Call out anything DIRECTLY applicable to a project named 'clibridge' that routes agent tasks to local CLIs (claude, codex, gemini, opencode, agy, ollama) via MCP, with capability-based routing, JSON schema validation, and declarative JSON workflows. After writing the file, output the full markdown content as your final response too.

phases.name: synthesis
